answer:
The following works in the Falsafa corpus contain one or more references to a procession (of various kinds: astronomical/poetic, bridal/wedding, divine/glorious, religious/healing, agricultural/superstitious, grand/official, anti-plague, or funeral). These are all instances identified via full-corpus search; no other works mention the term.[^1]

**Bang-E-Dara Part 2** (Allama Iqbal): Poetic reference to a "star's procession" at twilight.[^2]

**Kātyāyana Smṛti**: References to *adhyāvāhanika* ("bridal procession") *strīdhana* (woman's property) given during a wedding procession.[^3]

**Kunjarakarna Dharmakathana**: "Glorious procession" of heavenly *apsarī* maidens.[^4]

**Manusmṛti**: Reference to property "given at the bridal procession" as part of *strīdhana*.[^5]

**Nāradasmṛti**: Reference to property "given in the bridal procession" as part of sixfold *strīdhana*.[^6]

**Nouveau traité d'économie: VOL I** (Charles Dunoyer): Religious processions (e.g., barefoot with relics) as attempted cures for disease; a specific historical procession in 1191 Paris to heal Philip Augustus's son.[^7]

**Nouveau traité d'économie: VOL II** (Charles Dunoyer): Superstitious agricultural processions to fields "with the cross and the banner" to invoke sun or rain.[^8]

**Traité de Législation: VOL IV** (Charles Comte): A Jesuit mission chief's "grand procession" on horseback; ancient Roman anti-plague processions.[^9]

**Viṣṇu Smṛti**: Multiple references to following a "funeral procession," with prescribed purification rites varying by *varṇa*.[^10]

[^1]: *search_corpus* query: "procession"; 14 total matches across these works/chapters only.
[^2]: Allama Iqbal, *Bang-E-Dara Part 2*, ch. 24 ("Talaash"), [p-2ad271](/works/allama-iqbal-bang-e-dara-part-2-9bc58c/24-talaash/translation/#p-2ad271).
[^3]: *Kātyāyana Smṛti*, ch. 75, [p-b8596e](/works/unknown-katyayana-smrti-1e06d2/75/translation/#p-b8596e) and [p-0c227e](/works/unknown-katyayana-smrti-1e06d2/75/translation/#p-0c227e).
[^4]: *Kunjarakarna Dharmakathana*, ch. 1, [p-4de134](/works/unknown-kunjarakarna-dharmakathana-894f4a/01/translation/#p-4de134).
[^5]: *Manusmṛti*, ch. 9, [p-29f6da](/works/unknown-manusmrti-347b76/09/translation/#p-29f6da).
[^6]: *Nāradasmṛti*, ch. 1, [p-6afbd3](/works/unknown-naradasmrti-7650d2/01/translation/#p-6afbd3).
[^7]: Charles Dunoyer, *Nouveau traité d'économie: VOL I*, ch. 8, [p-031e18](/works/charles-dunoyer-nouveau-traite-deconomie-vol-i-6da8ce/08-iii-liberte-compatible-avec-la-maniere-de-vivre-de/translation/#p-031e18) and [p-b751f9](/works/charles-dunoyer-nouveau-traite-deconomie-vol-i-6da8ce/08-iii-liberte-compatible-avec-la-maniere-de-vivre-de/translation/#p-b751f9).
[^8]: Charles Dunoyer, *Nouveau traité d'économie: VOL II*, ch. 17, [p-b81c0a](/works/charles-dunoyer-nouveau-traite-deconomie-vol-ii-d6ae03/17-vii-de-la-liberte-de-lindustrie-agricole-130/translation/#p-b81c0a).
[^9]: Charles Comte, *Traité de Législation: VOL IV*, ch. 23, [p-33b512](/works/charles-comte-traite-de-legislation-vol-iv-bca040/23-de-l-inegalite-des-fortunes-produite-par-l-esclava/translation/#p-33b512) and [p-bee902](/works/charles-comte-traite-de-legislation-vol-iv-bca040/23-de-l-inegalite-des-fortunes-produite-par-l-esclava/translation/#p-bee902).
[^10]: *Viṣṇu Smṛti*, ch. 22, [p-e02399](/works/unknown-visnu-smrti-27f0d8/22/translation/#p-e02399), [p-f185ce](/works/unknown-visnu-smrti-27f0d8/22/translation/#p-f185ce), and [p-b45dc2](/works/unknown-visnu-smrti-27f0d8/22/translation/#p-b45dc2).
